proposals.reason: ISO/TS 24971-2 published; now the reference standard for ML/AI risk management in medical devices. Notified bodies already requesting compliance in MDR evaluations; QARA must adapt risk documentation by end Q3 2026.

card.t: ISO/TS 24971-2 — ML/AI Risk Management Guidance Published
card.x: Technical Specification detailing algorithm uncertainty, data drift, and real-world clinical validation for SaMD. Immediate reference for MDR compliance documentation.

proposals.reason: MDCG 2026-4 finalised; clarifies SSCP data entry, validation, and maintenance rules in EUDAMED. Manufacturers must verify and correct trial data by end August 2026 to avoid administrative suspension.

card.t: MDCG 2026-4 — SSCP Management in EUDAMED Finalised
card.x: Final guidance on clinical trial data (sponsor/CRO/investigator roles, certification workflows) by risk class. Compliance deadline end August 2026.

proposals.reason: MDCG mandates transition from legacy 'EC REP' to 'EU REP' on all labels, packaging, and product documentation. Affects all class II/III devices; stock audit and compliance timeline required.

card.t: EU REP Symbol Transition — EC REP Deprecated
card.x: Mandatory replacement of legacy 'EC REP' marking with 'EU REP' on labels, packaging, and IFU. Notified bodies verify compliance in 2026–2027 audits.

proposals.reason: Decision 2026/1231 adds new harmonised standards for MDR Classes II/III (imaging, sterilisation, cybersecurity). Manufacturers must verify applicability and update conformity matrices by end Q3 2026.

card.t: Decision 2026/1231 — New Harmonised Standards for MDR
card.x: Additions to MDR harmonised standards catalogue (imaging, sterilisation, cybersecurity). Presumption of conformity applies; QARA must evaluate applicability.

proposals.reason: MDCG 2021-24 Rev.1 (28 April 2026) clarifies classification thresholds; some Class II devices may reclassify to Class III. Manufacturers must audit and notify ANSM if reclassification detected by end 2026.

card.x: Revision clarifies sterile instrumentation, temporary implants >24h, and software-controlled devices. Potential reclassification Class II ↔ III; audit required by end 2026.

proposals.reason: EMDN v2026 revision (MDCG 2026-1/2/3) harmonises product codes for AI/SaMD and implants. Manufacturers must update EUDAMED codes by September 2026 (ANSM deadline); incorrect codes trigger rejection or suspension.

card.t: EMDN v2026 — Product Nomenclature Codes Updated
card.x: Revision aligns EMDN codes with AI/SaMD and implant category evolution. Deadline September 2026 for EUDAMED compliance; consultation with ANSM advised for hybrid/complex AI devices.

proposals.reason: prEN 18286 now confirmed for publication Q4 2026 (December target). First edition will be MDR harmonised standard for AI QMS; notified bodies expected to require compliance Q1 2027.

card.t: prEN 18286 — AI QMS Standard Publication Target (December 2026)
card.x: First harmonised standard for AI QMS (MDR + AI Act integration). Publication expected December 2026; adoption mandatory Q1 2027 for high-risk SaMD.

proposals.reason: MHRA AI Airlock Phase 2 report (February 2026) details validation best practices for 8–10 SaMD products. Emphasises training data traceability, real-world population validation, and post-market monitoring; aligns with ISO/CEN standards.

card.t: MHRA AI Airlock Phase 2 — SaMD Validation Best Practices
card.x: Pilot report on 8–10 SaMD (cancer diagnosis, sepsis prediction). Key findings: data traceability critical; real-world validation essential; continuous post-approval monitoring required.

proposals.reason: Swissmedic (Switzerland) and TGA (Australia) activate mandatory traceability via UDI (1 July 2026). CH uses risk-based model; AU exhaustive. Manufacturers exporting to both markets must adapt data mappings and meet local deadlines immediately.

card.t: Swissmedic & Australia TGA — Mandatory Traceability (UDI) Active
card.x: Switzerland (risk-based model) and Australia (exhaustive registry) both require UDI-harmonised traceability from 1 July 2026. Data schema diverges; market-specific adaptation required.
